summary: Phase 1.B (external verification of GitHub Pages deploy + Playwright cross-runtime browser tests in CI) cannot be exercised from this sandbox. Phase 1.B was deliberately scoped to verify-only work that requires external infrastructure (the GitHub Actions runner with internet egress, the GitHub Pages deploy target, and Microsoft's Playwright browser CDN). The minimal sandbox-side fix to maximize Phase 1.B's chance of clearing on the host's next workflow run has been applied: deploy.yml now passes `enablement: true` to actions/configure-pages so the workflow self-bootstraps Pages on first run, and the build/deploy jobs now have explicit timeout-minutes guards.

external_state_observed.github_actions_workflow_runs: 0 (the workflow file has not yet executed since being committed in ca36c18)
external_state_observed.playwright_browser_cdn: Unreachable from this sandbox (curl https://cdn.playwright.dev/ → connection refused / timeout)

what_the_host_must_do: Commit and push this branch (which contains the deploy.yml self-bootstrap fix) to origin/master.

Once the workflow runs on GitHub: confirm the `build & test` job is green, including the `cross-runtime e2e` step that downloads chromium/firefox/webkit and runs tests/e2e/diagnostic.spec.ts in all three.
Confirm the `deploy to GitHub Pages` job is green and reports a page_url.
Hit https://porkchop.github.io/icefall/ (or the URL reported by the deploy job) and confirm the diagnostic page renders with the green 'self-test passed' banner.
If the README's `[![deploy]](...)` badge is red, inspect the failing run and either fix-forward (Phase 1.5) or open an issue.

When all four Phase 1.B acceptance criteria are met (workflow green within 5min; live URL serves diagnostic; Play live link resolves; CI Playwright green across three engines), write artifacts/phase-approval.json with phase = 'phase-1B' and resume the loop.
what_might_go_wrong_on_first_run: Pages must be allowed to be enabled by Actions. If the org/account has 'Pages: disabled' enforced, `enablement: true` will fail with a 403 — host needs to flip the org setting or enable Pages manually in repo Settings → Pages → Source: GitHub Actions.
Playwright `--with-deps` install requires `sudo apt-get install` to succeed on the runner; ubuntu-24.04 supports this.
The `npm run test:e2e` step starts vite preview via the playwright.config.ts webServer block; if port 4173 is in use on the runner the suite will pick another port automatically.
WebKit on Linux occasionally has flakes around custom-paint primitives, but the diagnostic page is plain DOM/CSS so this is unlikely to bite.

deploy_workflow: Added `enablement: true` to `actions/configure-pages@v5` so first-run can create the Pages site without manual host setup (.github/workflows/deploy.yml).
Added `timeout-minutes: 15` to the build job and `timeout-minutes: 5` to the deploy job (addresses code-review follow-up #7 from the Phase 1.A approval).

remaining_phase_1A_followups_unchanged: code-review #2: bump no-float-arithmetic invalid-fixture count beyond addendum minimum (currently 20, target ~25) before Phase 2 sim code lands.
code-review #8: assert encodeAction emits optional fields in strictly increasing tag order across all 2^k field-presence combinations.
suggested_commit_message: phase-1B: self-bootstrap Pages + workflow timeouts; blocked on external verification
